note: Table-stakes for an embeddable, local-first, MCP-native memory layer for AI agents. Derived from the direct-cluster field scan (mnemo, ai-memory-mcp, perseus-vault, Memoria, sqlite-memory). Cloud/framework memory (mem0, letta, graphiti, cognee) is a separate cluster and not scored here.

capabilities.test: exposes memory as MCP tools over stdio JSON-RPC
fuses dense vector + keyword/FTS ranking (e.g. RRF)
ships a real in-process semantic embedding model (e.g. MiniLM) so semantic recall works out of the box, no external service
a new fact that conflicts with an old belief supersedes it, not just appends
recall is weighted by a recency/importance decay curve
stored memory is encrypted on disk
a single write persists incrementally, not by re-encoding/re-writing the whole store
a reproducible recall-quality number on a public memory eval (LongMemEval / LoCoMo)
one-command install plus docs, examples, quickstart
valid-time and/or transaction-time history, not just latest-value provenance
native client SDKs beyond the core language (Python/TS/Go)
a structurally-enforced tier whose content can never reach a cloud/remote model
core invariants machine-proven (mutation testing / formal), not just unit-tested
